rulings.Case Title: IN RE: BJ HERRAN REVOCABLE TR ESTABLISHED 4/13/2006
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Limiting Discovery [Ccp Â§2520.420]
rulings.Rulings: Charles Odenthal's Motion for Protective Order Limiting Discovery is denied. Odenthal has not shown good cause justifying the imposition of a protective order udner CCP Section 2025.420(b). The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling shall notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than March 4, 2015, at 4:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: BJ HERRAN REVOCABLE TR ESTABLISHED 4/13/2006
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records, Alternatively, To Enforce Deposition Subpoenas; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,860.00
rulings.Rulings: The motion to compel compliance with deposition subpoenas for production of business records is denied. (See C.C.P. Section 2025.280(b).) The motion to enforce deposition subpoenas is denied. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding Odenthal's "right to privacy" objection. Odenthal's objection based on "failure to properly name nonparty deponent" is overruled. The respective requests for sanctions are denied. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling shall notify counsel and opposing counsel no later than 3/4/2015, at 4:00 p.m.
